name: ElectrumX
description: ElectrumX server — indexes the Bitcoin blockchain and serves Electrum wallets over the Electrum protocol. Connects to your Bitcoin Core node over RPC.
compose:
name: "slashnode-electrumx"
services:
  electrumx:
    image: "lukechilds/electrumx:v1.16.0"
    container_name: "electrumx"
    restart: "unless-stopped"
    networks:
      - slashnode
    environment:
      DAEMON_URL: "http://${bitcoind.exports.rpc.user}:${bitcoind.exports.rpc.password}@${bitcoind.exports.rpc.host}:8332"
      COIN: "BitcoinSegwit"
      NET: "mainnet"
      DB_DIRECTORY: "/data"
      SERVICES: "tcp://0.0.0.0:50001,rpc://0.0.0.0:8000"
    ports:
      - "50001:50001"
    volumes:
      - "electrumx-data:/data"
networks:
  slashnode:
    external: true
volumes:
  electrumx-data:
    name: "slashnode-electrumx_electrumx_electrumx-data"
notes: An Electrum server (electrs-compatible). Point your Electrum wallet at it on port 50001 (clearnet host:50001 or the app's .onion). Initial indexing of the full chain takes a long time and needs a fully-synced Bitcoin Core with txindex. It syncs over RPC, so no extra bitcoind config is needed.
